instruction:
Write a complete testthat test file for `httr2::check_content_type`.

Package: httr2 v1.2.2.9000
Description: Perform HTTP Requests and Process the Responses
Function: check_content_type
Parameters: content_type, valid_types, valid_suffix, inform_check_type, call
Test patterns used: expect_snapshot, test_that
Expectations used: expect_no_error, expect_snapshot

Function Code:
```r
check_content_type <- function(
  content_type,
  valid_types = NULL,
  valid_suffix = NULL,
  inform_check_type = FALSE,
  call = caller_env()
) {
  parsed <- parse_content_type(content_type)
  base_type <- paste0(parsed$type, "/", parsed$subtype)

  if (is.null(valid_types) || base_type %in% valid_types) {
    return()
  }
  if (!is.null(valid_suffix) && parsed$suffix == valid_suffix) {
    return()
  }

  msg <- "Expecting type {.or {.str {valid_types}}}"
  if (!is.null(valid_suffix)) {
    msg <- paste0(msg, " or suffix {.str {valid_suffix}}.")
  }

  cli::cli_abort(
    c("Unexpected content type {.str {content_type}}.", "*" = msg),
    i = if (inform_check_type) "Override check with `check_type = FALSE`.",
    call = call
  )
}
```

Requirements:
1. Use test_that() blocks with descriptive names that reference `check_content_type`
2. Test basic functionality using appropriate expectations (expect_no_error, expect_snapshot)
3. Test edge cases: NULL input, empty strings, missing arguments
4. Test error conditions with expect_error()
5. Each test_that() block should test one logical behavior
reference_test:
expect_snapshot(
    check_content_type("application/json", "application/xml"),
    error = TRUE
  )

expect_snapshot(
    check_content_type("application/test+json", "application/xml", "xml"),
    error = TRUE
  )
